selected_hypotheses.summary: Multiple feature pairs exhibit near-perfect or perfect correlations (correlation=1 or ~0.9993), indicating strong linear dependencies and potential redundancy among features related to packet lengths, segment sizes, flags, and packet counts.
selected_hypotheses.reason: Strong perfect correlations suggest critical redundancy impacting feature independence and model robustness.

selected_hypotheses.summary: Destination Port feature shows strong distributional differences between BENIGN and DDoS classes, with the DDoS class concentrated on a single port (dominant ratio=1.0, zero entropy), indicating a representation-sensitive pattern likely related to attack targeting.
selected_hypotheses.reason: Distinct class-conditioned port concentration reveals a key representation-sensitive pattern with high investigative value.

selected_hypotheses.summary: There is a tension between features exhibiting near-constant values (low cardinality) such as bulk and flag features, and other features showing strong class-conditioned structural variation and distributional shifts, suggesting heterogeneous feature behavior within the dataset.
selected_hypotheses.reason: The coexistence of near-constant and highly variable features presents a fundamental tension affecting feature utility and model design.

deferred_hypotheses.summary: Backward bulk-related features (Bwd Avg Bulk Rate, Bwd Avg Bytes/Bulk, Bwd Avg Packets/Bulk, Bwd PSH Flags, Bwd URG Flags) exhibit near-constant values with unique value counts equal to 1 and very low cardinality ratios, suggesting a structural uniformity or saturation effect in backward traffic bulk characteristics.

Forward bulk-related features (Fwd Avg Bulk Rate, Fwd Avg Bytes/Bulk, Fwd Avg Packets/Bulk, Fwd URG Flags) mirror the backward bulk features in showing extremely low cardinality and unique values equal to 1, indicating a similar structural uniformity or saturation in forward traffic bulk metrics.
The CWE Flag Count feature shows near-constant values with unique value count equal to 1 and very low cardinality ratio, suggesting minimal variation in this flag count across the dataset partition.

Backward packet length features (Bwd Packet Length Max, Mean, Std, Avg Bwd Segment Size) show substantial class-conditioned structural variation, with differing unique value counts and variance ratios between BENIGN and DDoS classes, indicating these features may capture meaningful class-distinguishing information.

Average Packet Size exhibits class-conditioned structural variation with moderate variance ratio and differing unique value counts between BENIGN and DDoS classes, suggesting this feature may reflect behavioral differences between classes.

A tension exists between the observed perfect correlations among many feature pairs and the presence of duplicated rows in the partition data, raising questions about data quality and feature independence.

The Fwd PSH Flags and SYN Flag Count features form a perfect correlation pair but individually have low cardinality with unique values equal to 1 and very low cardinality ratio, indicating a potentially trivial but structurally concentrated signal within forward flag features.
